behavior_summary: On each rising edge of clk the 4-bit register count either resets to 0 if reset=1, increments by one modulo 16 when enable=1, or holds its previous value when enable=0 and reset=0.
edge_cases: Wrap-around: when count reaches 4'b1111 and enable stays high with reset low, the next increment wraps count to 4'b0000.
Reset priority: reset overrides enable on the same clock edge, so count is cleared even if enable=1.
Hold behavior: when enable=0 and reset=0, count must remain unchanged across clock edges; no combinational glitches described.
state_transitions: count=N -> count=0 on rising clk if reset=1 (N is any 0-15).
count=N -> count=(N+1) mod 16 on rising clk if reset=0 and enable=1.
count=N -> count=N on rising clk if reset=0 and enable=0.
priorities: reset > enable > hold (implicit when both reset and enable asserted).
